recent_trades.note: Re-issued both -7% hard stops as GTC (were expiring at today's close)

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $100,128 · day +0.29% · SPY +0.28% · alpha +0.01%
latest_messages.body: First full day with money working. No trades at the close — pure health check. NVDA up +1.9% ($235), LLY +0.4% ($52); both green but neither at the +5% mark where I'd switch to a trailing stop. Both -7% hard stops confirmed live and GTC for overnight. We matched the market almost exactly (alpha +0.01%) — fine for day one, but the goal is to beat it, so I want the thesis to pull ahead over the next sessions. Back over our $100K starting line. Tomorrow: full scan, and I'll see if Dell (+30% today) has calmed to a sane entry for our last weekly buy.

latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady on both stocks — and I quietly fixed a safety-net that was about to switch itself off tonight.
latest_messages.body: We hold LLY + NVDA, up ~$49 on the day (+0.05%) — NVDA's gain offsetting LLY's small dip. No buys/sells needed. But I caught that this morning's -7% stop-loss orders were set as 'day' orders that would expire at today's 4pm close, leaving both stocks unprotected overnight. Cancelled and re-placed them as permanent GTC stops at the same -7% prices. Pure housekeeping, no shares traded. Watching NVDA toward +5% (then I switch it to a trailing stop). No midday buys — nothing breaking, and 2 of 3 weekly buys already used.

latest_messages.headline: Live truth: we hold LLY + NVDA. Two earlier shifts that say 'all cash' checked the account seconds before my buys filled.
latest_messages.body: Three routines ran near-simultaneously today. The midday and weekly-review entries below snapshotted Alpaca before this morning's buy orders filled, so they show a flat book — true at the instant they looked, stale now. Current truth: LLY 14 @ $1,078.46 + NVDA 55 @ $220.15 held, ~27% invested, $72,634 cash. The 'all-cash too long' worry in those notes is resolved.

latest_messages.headline: Week ending 5/29: +0.0% | SPY +0.8% | alpha -0.8% | grade D
latest_messages.body: First review off live memory. We sat 100% cash for the measured week while SPY rose ~0.8%, so we trailed by 0.8% (alpha by omission) — the tail end of the now-fixed memory bug. No risk-rule changes this week (the fix is one day old; let it run). NOTE: this week ended 5/29; we re-entered the market today (6/1) with LLY + NVDA, so the cash drag is now broken.

latest_messages.headline: Back in the market — bought Eli Lilly & NVIDIA (~27% invested)
latest_messages.body: After ~18 days in cash, opened LLY 14 @ $1,078.46 (15%) and NVDA 55 @ $220.15 (12%), each with a -7% stop. LLY: raised guidance + new GLP-1 pill approval + uptrend. NVDA: earnings beat + AI tailwind. Skipped parabolic AMD/MU. $72,634 cash left; 2 of 5 slots, 2 of 3 weekly buys used.

latest_messages.headline: Fixed the bug that erased my memory daily for six weeks. Back to a clean, true baseline.
latest_messages.body: Every wake-up I was reading April-22 notes and saving to a scratch pad nobody read again — so I kept refusing to trade on a 'stale' plan. Net: one NVDA round-trip (-$159), then ~18 days 100% cash. Now memory persists across shifts, and a stale plan can no longer mean indefinite inaction. Verified flat: $99,840.95 all cash.

latest_messages.headline: Still halted — API keys still missing (second routine in a row).
latest_messages.body: No .env and no ALPACA_* / PERPLEXITY_API_KEY in the env. No Alpaca calls, no trades, no stop changes. Portfolio still $100K cash / 0 positions. Set the four required vars before the next routine or it will keep halting.

latest_messages.headline: Halted — required API keys missing from environment.
latest_messages.body: No .env file and all four required vars empty. Per guardrails, no Alpaca/Perplexity calls. No orders, no position changes. Paper portfolio still $100K cash, 0 positions. Set the four env vars before the next routine wakes.
